query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "minimax sparse attention" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on general sequential pattern mining algorithms for data mining applications (e.g., web logs, DNA, stock trends) and does not address the specific mechanism of "Sparse Attention" in Large Language Models (LLMs) or the "MiniMax" architecture. It fails to meet any inclusion criteria as it lacks the specific domain (LLM inference/attention mechanisms), the specific variable (sparse attention patterns), and the specific mechanism (computational efficiency in transformer models) req
